page.calculator.title: Lost-cart revenue calculator for Shopify | Persistent Cart
page.calculator.desc: A transparent, illustrative estimate of revenue lost when signed-in shoppers switch devices on your Shopify store. Adjust the benchmarks; we show the formula.
page.calculator.h1: How much could cross-device cart loss be costing you?
page.calculator.lede: When a signed-in shopper builds a cart on one device and finishes on another, a default Shopify store loses it. This calculator turns that into a rough, illustrative range — using your numbers and industry benchmarks. Every input is editable and the formula is shown; nothing is a black box.
calc.heading: Estimate your lost-cart revenue
calc.intro: Defaults are illustrative starting points (industry benchmarks where they exist). Adjust them to match your store.
calc.input.orders.label: Monthly orders
calc.input.orders.help: Roughly how many orders your store gets per month.
calc.input.aov.label: Average order value
calc.input.loggedIn.label: Share of shoppers signed in
calc.input.loggedIn.help: Portion of buyers who shop while logged into an account. We default to 50% as an illustrative starting point — set it to your store's reality.
calc.input.crossDevice.label: Share who switch devices
calc.input.crossDevice.help: Portion of signed-in shoppers who browse across phone and desktop before buying. Benchmark: ~30% (reasoned estimate, not a measured rate).
calc.input.recovery.label: Carts saved that become orders
calc.input.recovery.help: Conservative share of those cross-device journeys that turn into an order they'd otherwise have lost. Default 2% — deliberately cautious.
calc.currency.label: Currency
calc.result.heading: Illustrative recovered revenue
calc.result.rangeNote: Shown as a range (±) because this is an estimate, not a measured result.
calc.result.disclaimer: This is a rough illustrative range based on your inputs and industry averages — not a measured result from the app. The device-switch → lost-sale link is a reasoned inference, not a published rate. Treat it as a directional sense of scale.
calc.formula.label: The formula (no black box)
calc.formula.text: Recovered orders/month = Monthly orders × Signed-in share × Device-switch share × Carts-saved rate. Revenue = recovered orders × AOV. Annual = × 12.
calc.cta.lead: Want us to check your actual store instead of benchmarks?
calc.cta.install: Add Persistent Cart — free for 30 days
calc.page.s1.h: Why a default Shopify store leaks cross-device carts
calc.page.s1.b: On a standard Shopify store the cart is stored in a browser cookie, not on the customer's account. So a signed-in shopper's cart doesn't follow them from phone to laptop — it simply isn't there on the second device. The shopper either rebuilds a smaller cart from memory or doesn't return at all. Because no error is shown and no email is triggered, most merchants never see the loss in their reports.
calc.page.s2.b: The calculator multiplies your order volume by three editable shares: how many shoppers are signed in, how many of those move between devices, and a deliberately conservative rate for how many of those journeys a kept cart turns into a sale. We show the result as a range, never a single confident number, because the device-switch-to-lost-sale link is a reasoned inference rather than a measured statistic. The honest version of this math is more credible than a borrowed "+X% sales" claim — and it's why we show the formula.
calc.page.s3.b: An estimate is a starting point. To see whether your specific store drops signed-in carts across devices — and what that's worth for you — request a free store audit. We'll check your store's actual cross-device behavior and send back what we find, with no obligation and no install required.
